title: Heyday: Peach & Prosumer
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Heyday is a queer night for people passionate about house music with Prosumer, this time with special guest Peach.

Prosumer decamped to Edinburgh from Berlin where he helped shape the sound Berghain’s Panorama Bar alongside long time collaborator Steffi. Now a popular NTS Radio resident, he’s continued to tour globally and is now equally well known as a frequent flyer at Homobloc, Glastonbury’s Block9 and NYC Downlow, Love International and numerous Boiler Rooms.

A staple at queer parties including NYC Downlow, Homobloc and Maricas (Barcelona), Peach's sets have become beloved worldwide for the emotional range and bubbly energy that radiates directly from her person & her musical choices. Her style is classic, yet steeped in modern flair, inhabiting a continuum of sound that can be defined by the uplifting energy that is felt through her sets.

title: Alien Disko: Tanukichi (Night On Earth / RAM Records / Undergroundtekno)
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Alien Disko returns to our residency at the Bongo Club on Friday 27th February with our first international booking of the year: the legendary Tanukichi!

Tanukichi is a Japanese DJ who’s always willing to evolve and to experiment at the frontiers of sound, in order to craft original tunes that strike powerful chords with his audience.

He has been into Europe’s free party culture since 2006, after a life-changing Teknival experience in his home country. Since then, he has been producing music that perfectly fits this scene’s vibe. Over the years he became one of the faces of European Hardtek, as well as being a trait d’union between Japanese and European rave scenes.

Tanukichi’s vivacious sound, centered on powerful kick drums, big bass and steady rhythms, all matched with a pristine and flawless sound production, moves and contaminates the audience’s spirit in a chain of energy and entertainment.

He is currently trying to achieve an high-powered sound with 4 kicks and a 175 BPM pace, pioneering the next era of drum and bass, energising people and making them dance, feel, and fly on the high spirits of the kick bass, as has always been the case in Tanukichi’s 18 years long music career.

Tanukichi consistently performs at major festivals across Europe and Japan.
Among others, he has played: Boomtown Festival and Glastonbury in the UK, Fujirock in Japan, Defqon.1 Festival in the Netherlands, Electrobotik Invasion and  Reperkusound in France, Selvatek in Spain, Beats For Love Festival and X-Massacre in Czech Republic.

As a producer who's music is always a staple of our residents USBs, we are very excited to have Tanukichi for his Bongo headline debut! 

Joining Tanukichi in the booth we're delighted to announce that we have Jigsore's Karl Radox and Brockout's duo Rhythm Session venturing up north, who will be spanning 140-165bpm Acid, Jungle, and Breaks. Alien Disko residents will be completing the roster with our signature multi-genre style.

4X4 / DNB / HARDTEK / ACID / TEKNO / JUNGLE / BREAKS

ANNEX arrives at The Wee Red Bar on Friday 27th February for a late-night session. Running from 23:00–03:00, the night will move across Hard House, Garage and Tech House, blending fast-paced grooves, weighty basslines and rolling rhythms into one continuous flow. This isn’t about sticking to one sound, it’s about reading the room, pushing the tempo, and keeping the dancefloor active from open to close.   

You can expect a wide variety with some Tech and Hard house royalty being spun, featuring tracks from Morgan Seatree, Dart, DJ Heartstring, KETTAMA and many more. Matching the same fast-paced energetic sound will be countless Garage legends, with the likes of Interplanetary Criminal, Sammy Virgi, Silva Bumpa and Soul Mass Transit System - blending seamlessly into a night that pushes the passion for atmosphere, movement and energy.  
 
If you’re into high tempos, bouncing dancefloors and freedom for the night, this one’s for you.

title: Session Victim (LIVE) ⥊ Lobos ⥊ Hobbes ⥊ Jedda ⥊ Mixfits ⥊ Geordie ⥊ Club_Nacht
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
For our second guest of the season, we’re delighted to finally welcome a long-standing bucket-list booking and one of Germany’s most respected electronic acts.... Join us as Berlin’s Session Victim arrive in the Scottish capital for a very special live appearance on Saturday 28 February.

Renowned for pushing boundaries within house and electronic music, Session Victim describe themselves, with typical understatement, as a “Two Man House Band”. It’s an fitting title given their sound is rich, warm, deeply musical and built on feeling and meaninful groove.

Hauke Freer and Matthias Reiling are widely regarded as one of the most accomplished house duos of their generation. Their catalogue includes three genre-defining triple LPs on UK tastemaker label Delusions of Grandeur, alongside the critically acclaimed Needledrop album for Night Time Stories: a release praised for its depth, musicality and reverence for dancefloor culture. Add to this over twenty vinyl-only EPs and a string of modern classics for labels such as Rhythm Section, Retreat and Pen & Paper, and their influence becomes difficult to overstate.

As performers, there are very few modern house acts who havee logged as much meaningful club time as our February guests. For nearly two decades now, the Berlin-based duo have been quietly - and sometimes not so quietly - left their mark on dancefloors across the globe, from sweat-soaked basements to some of the most respected clubs and festivals in electronic music (think Panaroma Bar, Fabric, De School, Corsica to name but a few!).

It’s this extensive, hard-earned road experience that gives Session Victim their unmistakable confidence behind the machines they use.

Don't miss this rare opportunity to catch them live in an indepdent club setting. Tickets on sale now! 

⥊

2026 Parties:

Sat 03 Jan ⥊ Club_Nacht Payback Party
Sat 31 Jan ⥊ Étienne de Crécy (Super Discount) - WARM UP Album Tour
Sat 28 Feb ⥊ Session Victim [Live]

Sat 18 July ⥊ Bongo Closing Party w/Special Guest TBA

+ many more to come

title: Samedia x Oi Musica: Dance Into Light feat. Jamz Supernova, El Buho & More
location: Portobello Town Hall, 147-149 Portobello High St, Edinburgh EH15 1AF
description:
Saturday 28th February
Samedia Shebeen & Oi Musica present

DANCE INTO LIGHT
DAY PARTY: 5pm - 11pm
With
Jamz Supernova (DJ Set)
El Buho (Live)
Oi Musica (Live)
+ Samedia DJs

Portobello Town Hall
Edinburgh

Plus: Samedia Decor / Decades of Dub Bar / Knights Kitchen African Food / Family Friendly

Legendary Edinburgh tropical party starters Samedia Shebeen join forces with street music promoters and educators Oi Musica for a very special one-off celebration in the heart of Portobello where both outfits are based.

Jamz Supernova (BBC 6music / DJ Set)
Jamz Supernova uses sonics to tell a story. The multi-faceted creative force is a label head, radio host, DJ, podcaster, and true tastemaker, broadcasting for over a decade. Known for her slots on BBC Radio 1Xtra (Best Specialist Aria Gold Winner 2021), BBC 6Music (Broadcast & Press Guild Best radio show of 2022) & Selector Radio for the British Council, reaching over 4 million global listeners. She is an avid explorer, collector and promoter of diverse worldwide sounds and frequently travels the globe to connect and understand musical movements at their source. As a DJ she weaves these thread together with style and skill and plenty of attitude to make a great dancefloor experience for all.

El Buho (Shike Shika / Live)
Robin Perkins aka El Búho was born in the rolling hills of northern England but found his musical home in the forests, rivers & mountains of Latin America. His sound marries his influence of Dub, IDM and Electronica with the rhythms, traditions and melodies of Latin American folk and the organic sound of waterfalls, birdsongs and crackling leaves. The result is a dreamy, deep, melodic journey that entrances as much through headphones as it does on the dancefloor.

His breakthrough EP Cenotes, written whilst living in Mexico and featuring the track Mañana Tepotzlan, was released in 2015 via Wonderwheel Recordings and was followed in 2017 by his debut album Balance to critical acclaim as one of the the leading names in the emerging organic electronic scene. 

Alongside his music, El Búho is also an environmental activist, having worked for over eight years at Greenpeace, being a member of DJs For Climate Action and coordinating the non-profit project “A Guide to the Birdsong” a series of albums that raise funds and awareness for endangered bird species through electronic music. In 2020 he also participated in the New York Times author Ian Urbina’s project The Outlaw Ocean, creating a four track EP inspired by the ocean deep and the need to protect this mysterious, beautiful and fragile ecosystem from threats like oil drilling.

Oi Musica (Live)
Oi Musica creates visceral live performances using rhythm, percussion and brass. Their music is all about building energy and blurring the lines between audience and performer. They'll be debuting a brand new project with live brass & percussion, fat electronics and plenty of vibe! Drawing inspiration from global street band music, Oi Musica is guaranteed to bring the energy of hot dusty streets at carnival time right into the heart of the dance floor. Don’t miss the debut of Oi Musica’s freshest new act…

Samedia DJs
This crew have been rocking dancefloors across the UK and beyond for over 15 years with their exciting fusions of world and global club sounds. Known locally for their annual Solsctice Beach Party and long-running residency at the Mash House.

North Fire Soundsystem
Providing the bass, mids and tops all the way from Cumbria - one of the top soundsystems on the scene and long time collaborator with Samedia at Knockengorroch, Boomtown and beyond.

This event runs from 5pm - 11pm. Under 18s are welcome until 8pm.

Tickets on sale now!

title: ICE OUT! Minneapolis
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
Import x Slàinte Mhath are joining forces to fundraise towards supporting the people affected and efforts to resist ICE in Minneapolis. The city has been under intense policing since the murder of Renee Good, with ICE continuing their operation, terrifying and harassing immigrants from all backgrounds, disabled people, as well as residents alike. The people of Minneapolis need our help as they rally to aid those most impacted. 

ICE OUT! Minneapolis takes place at Peoples Leisure Club on March 1st 7pm-12am to unify in a common goal of protecting the city of Minneapolis and those impacted by ICE. We have hand-picked some of our favorite DJs to play a mix of genres throughout the whole night.  We hope you will join us in showing that Edinburgh stands in solidarity with Minneapolis and against ICE. Immigrants make our communities better! 

Tickets are available via RA and on the door with all ticket proceeds going towards the chosen charity. Optional extra donations are encouraged on both ticketing platforms as well as on the door.

Funds raised through this event will go directly to support families with basic needs and wellness care, including rent, groceries, utilities, and other urgent expenses. You can have a look at our chosen Mutual Aid fund below in the fundraiser link!

title: ELEMENTS: Chapter 2 - Fire
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
ELEMENTS: Chapter 2 - Fire 
Elements returns for Chapter 2: Fire, and this time we’re turning the heat up!

Following our opening Water chapter, Fire brings more energy, more vibes, warmer tempos and a room full of movement. The night lands just before International Women’s Day, so we’re celebrating with an all-female DJ lineup, shining a light on selectors doing brilliant things across the Scottish scene.

Expect the space to be fully transformed again! Red, orange and yellow tones, brand-new custom artwork, immersive lighting and fire twirlers. The room changes but message is the same: bringing that festival feeling indoors, just with a bit more heat this time.

On the decks all night are the daring duo PASO (Fly, Cabaret Voltaire) and Monicat (Sneaky Pete’s, Mash House), bringing fiery house selections from start to finish. These selectors have been tearing it up all across the country and we can’t wait to have them at Elememts. No headliners, just great DJs and great music all night long.

DRESS CODE:
Wear something red, orange or yellow...a tee, jacket, shades, anything. Keep it simple or go all out, we love both.
Chapter 2 is about energy, confidence and celebrating women behind the decks. If you enjoyed Water, expect Fire to hit a little harder.

title: feeo (live) at Pianodrome
location: Pianodrome Bruntsfield Community Hub, 41 Montpelier Park, Edinburgh EH10 4NB
description:
AD93 signee feeo comes to the Pianodrome on March 6th, touring her new album Goodness.

'One of the most breathtakingly beautiful albums of the year.' - Pitchfork

Feeo is the solo project of Theodora Laird, emerging from the London experimental improvisation scene. It's easy to get lost in Goodness' dense and cold soundscapes-- the album is a peculiar and tremendously compelling combination of the Copenhagen sound-dream, girded with a real immersive dread, reminiscent of F# A# Infinity at full pelt.

Every track is a highlight, but pay particular attention to 'win!'. The simple refrain of 'we can figure this out' is manipulated endlessly by Laird's shapeshifting voice; taking on new meanings with each dip, pause and elongation of the phrase. It feels like a memory of a dub song, the drums breaking through from time to time but firmly outside of the song; sounds are processed until they're unrecognisable then buzz around like insects. Finally, there's a moment of air before it all collapses into muffled bass.

Since the Album's release in October it has been subject to a steady stream of praise: Achieving #6 on Pitchfork's electronic albums of the year, DJ Mag's top albums of 2025 and the Guardian's best new tracks along with countless glowing reviews in publications across the music world.

Support comes from Daksh.

No one turned away due to lack of funds, free and concession tickets available, email office@curvingtrack.com

title: Night Tube X RAG // CHARITY CLUB NIGHT
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Night Tube and Edinburgh RAG are proud to welcome Bristol-based visionary Yushh to Edinburgh for a club night in aid of Alzheimer’s Research UK and Street Assist Edinburgh. 
Yushh represents the forefront of the contemporary UK underground dance scene.
As the founder of the seminal Pressure Dome label, Yushh has established a unique sonic identity characterised by polyrhythms and dynamic momentum. Her releases on Wisdom Teeth, Timedance, Rhythm Section and her own label Pressure Dome, balance ethereal synth textures with the raw, carnal power of humming basslines.

Known for a emotive and percussive approach that masterfully plays with halftime structures, Yushh curates a dancefloor experience that is innovative and movement focused.

Fresh from the success of Brushrunner (her collaborative project with Breaka and Dubrunner), she brings a technical versatility honed through a Noods Radio residency and international appearances at Berghain’s REEF, Draaimolen, and Freerotation.
Join us for a masterclass in the UK’s innovative dance lineage, where dubby precision meets high-intensity club aesthetics.

title: Jitwam (DJ set): Free Time
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Shaped by over a decade of moving through London, NYC, and Australia, Jitwam’s soulful music has always resisted borders. 

The Indian-born creative has long been known for nomadically fusing psychedelic soul, jazz, and house into spiritually charged records. Since his debut “TJD001” in 2013, Jitwam quickly gained respect from tastemakers like Gilles Peterson and Moodymann, who included the EP’s  “Keepyourbusinesstoyourself” in the now iconic DJ-Kicks compilation; a move that sparked international attention and set Jitwam on a global trajectory. 

With a creative process that is both intentional and intuitive, he released his self-titled debut album, ज़ितम सिहँ, in 2017. This was followed by several EPs on his own imprint, The Jazz Diaries, and full-length albums Honeycomb (2019) and Third (2022). With over 100 million streams to date, Honeycomb remains a defining release in Jitwam’s catalogue. 

Jitwam stands, as always, as a rare connective tissue between club rhythms and spiritual introspection. All part of his lived experience, his forthcoming project "ALLIN" project draws equal parts from classic soul, Indian cinema, lounge jazz, and Detroit house, a music community he holds a profound affinity for.

location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
27/03/26 - Metalheadz Edinburgh: Doc Scott, Benny L, & Ant TC1

We’re proud to announce that the legendary Metalheadz are back in Scotland for one night only! With three very special guests Doc Scott, Benny L, and Ant TC1 completing the lineup at The Bongo Club. If you caught the last Metalheadz party in February, you already know what’s about to go down! 

Support from father-and-son b2b: Simon Jackson b2b Harry Jackson.

*4am license TBC. 


Doc Scott artist bio: 
A true veteran and leading tastemaker in drum & bass, Doc Scott has been an essential and pivotal figure in the scene since before its inception and continues to push things forward. Founding member of ‘A Bunch Of Cuts’, his DJ sets in the mid-90s became legendary, notably at the Metalheadz Blue Note sessions and ‘Speed’. Although primarily a DJ, his productions are definitive classics within the scene, such as ‘Here Comes The Drumz’ on Reinforced, the first ever Metalheadz release ‘VIP Drumz’, to the more mellow sounds of ‘Tokyo Dawn’ on Good Looking Records.  His own label 31 Records, sparked by Scott’s ground-breaking ‘Shadow Boxing’, has also been a hub for new talent, pushing some of the biggest artists today in their early days. Having recently re-launched the label, Om Unit’s remix of ‘Shadow Boxing’ is the first release of 2014, with many others to follow. The New Year also marks a fresh start for Doc Scott, as he makes his long awaited return to the studio. 

Benny L artist bio: 
Growing up, music was the center point of Benny’s life. After learning a range of instruments, including drums, the piano, trumpet, and even the violin, he dedicated some of his most formative years to perfecting his craft. Although this could only be expected, as he grew up alongside family members who also devoted just as much time to honing their own musicality. But it was his older brother who eventually led him to an interest in DJing. Benny spent hours watching him mix in his bedroom, spinning everything from Jungle to Garage. Benny also began to seek out pirate radio stations in his local area, helping him develop his own musical tastes, which only grew as he got older. His brother would regularly head down to Swag Records and Big Apple Records in Croydon, with Benny waiting patiently for his return to hear about his weekly exploits. And when Benny eventually started purchasing his own music, he laid his hands on the Drum & Bass Arena ‘The Classics’ Compilation, mixed by Goldie in 2005, as well as Dillinja’s Cybotron album, which blew his ears away upon first listen.

title: Deep Excursions (Daytime): Maurice Fulton
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
Excited to welcome the supremely talented Maurice Fulton from Tokyo for the 2nd daytime instalment following the very lively & well received launch party with Linkwood & Craig Smith in December.

Originally from the port city of Baltimore in the U.S., he is widely regarded as one of house music's true originals with a back catalogue of highly sophisticated productions. A somewhat elusive character, his musical foundations run deep — shaped by disco, funk, soul and early hip-hop before becoming synonymous with house music in the early 90’s. As an extremely accomplished DJ, his sound is very much influenced by his background with his sets moving freely between house, disco, techno and left-field selections.

The intimate People's Leisure Club basement is the ideal setting to showcase his talents. 

Also excited to welcome Atjazz Record Company affiliated 'Peacey' to the lineup. In addition to being an all round top lad, he is, like Maurice Fulton, an extremely talented producer with a very impressive back catalogue of original productions and remixes whilst also being equally at home on the decks as he is in the studio.

Expect deepness and when it comes to Maurice Fulton, definitely expect the unexpected!

***Capacity limited to 200***

--------------------------------------------------------------------

Maurice Fulton Bio

Maverick production genius Maurice Fulton is a man of few words, who is more inclined to let his music do the talking. We know for certain that he comes from Baltimore in the USA and has been DJing since his early teens. Starting on Hip-hop and then moving on to House and Garage at various residencies across the US he played under his nickname DR Scratch.

Maurice began playing organ in church and drums in high school. So when he put his talent to making music it wasn’t long before he was using his skills to produce tracks with The Basement Boys and Ultra Nate. His most famous work was heard on Gypsy Woman by Crystal Waters, one of the most successful house tunes of the early ‘90s.

Maurice went on to become highly prolific in his production work releasing tracks on various labels through the 90’s including Nuphonic and Warp in the UK. Often linked to contemporaries like Carl Craig, Derrick May and Frankie Knuckles, his textured sounds and complexity of production astounded many and grew him a loyal fan base. Some called it weird house others arty disco, his left of centre productions were always on the money.

In his more recent years he has been working for Tirk, Kathy Diamond and DFA. Maurice likes to be known first and foremost as a producer, though for those in the know, Maurice is also an extremely accomplished DJ. His sets are firmly routed in the Loft and Garage classics that gave New York City its clubbing heritage. These tracks often quietly edited to perfection by his own hand, and presented and eq’ed to maximum effect on the dance floor.

Maurice Fulton is one of house music’s true originals and his enviable back catalogue will live long in a modern world of disposable downloads.

--------------------------------------------------------------------

Mike Huckaby (RIP)
Sven Weisemann
Tama Sumo
Rainer Truby
Domenic Cappello
Telfort
Craig Smith
Linkwood

title: Theo Kottis ⥊ Good Coll B2B Hobbes ⥊ DRUMA ⥊ Club Cadence  ⥊ Club_Nacht ⥊ Hobbes Music
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
This March, we’re delighted to welcome DJ, producer and long-time Rinse FM resident Theo Kottis back to Edinburgh, as he plays The Bongo Club for the very first time.

You’d have to be living under a rock not to know Theo. One of Scotland’s brightest exports, he’s long been a mainstay of the UK electronic music circuit and a trusted presence behind the decks, playing clubs and festivals across Europe. Despite his international reach, he remains deeply connected to the city’s club culture, regularly appearing at some of Edinburgh’s most respected parties.

Kicking off his DJ career at just eighteen, we first met Theo when he was throwing parties and co-running the electronic music–influenced clothing brand We Own alongside Marty Bell (Poolsuite) and Tom Ketley (Fly Open Air). Momentum built quickly: from grassroots parties across Scotland, the trio went on to bring BBC Radio 1’s Pete Tong to a sold-out Ocean Terminal Shopping Centre, before Theo secured a coveted main-stage slot at RockNess - Scotland’s largest festival after T in the Park - all within the space of a year.

Fast forward fifteen years and Theo has gone from strength to strength, taking his sound worldwide and playing clubs and festivals across Europe and beyond - from intimate basement rooms to major stages. Along the way, he’s amassed millions of streams through a steady run of releases on labels including Dekmantel, Fabric, FUSE, CWPT, Moda Black and Permanent Vacation, alongside remixes for artists such as Sasha, Goldfrapp, Joe Goddard (Hot Chip) and Shadow Child. 

Never fixed to one side, Kottis' sound has evolved, while remaining underground and consistently ahead of the curve.

title: Temporal - The 00s
location: The Liquid Room, 9c Victoria Street; Edinburgh, EH1 2HE; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
The timeline moves forward. Following the launch of our archival residency, Temporal returns to The Liquid Room to turn the page on the millennium. We continue our mission to document and relive the distinct eras of House.

As the world crossed into the new millennium, the sound of House evolved. For Chapter 2, we are moving from the raw roots of the 90s into the digital revolution and genre-bending energy of the 2000s. We aren't just looking for the superclub anthems; we are digging for the Electro-clash cuts, the French Touch gems, and the early Tech House records that redefined the dancefloor.

The Liquid Room was born in the 90s, but we grew up in the 00s. This was the decade where our walls soaked up the sweat of a changing scene. By bringing Temporal into this era, we are revisiting the nights that solidified our legacy as an Edinburgh institution.

Expect top-tier DJs and Selectors delving deep into the depths of your temporal lobe to bring the distinct, driving sound of 00s House back to the forefront.

title: Tierhaus at Summerhall Newington (artists TBA)
location: Summerhall, 1 Summerhall, Edinburgh, EH9 1PL
description:
Having only just recovered from our debut, Tierhaus returns to Summerhall, continuing our vision of creating a new kind of club night, in a new kind of club, built by Edinburgh, for Edinburgh.

Inspired by seminal 90s electronic music culture, Tierhaus aims to bring the communal spirit of 90s rave and dance music culture into 2026. Expect soulful, groovey house music rooted in 90s club culture with driving basslines and vocal hooks built for long, ecstastic moments, all in a unique setting at the heart of Edinburgh’s infamous Summerhall arts complex.

Alongside the music, Tierhaus will unfold as a multi-art, immersive, synaesthetic experience shaped by contributions from across the Edinburgh creative scene, featuring visuals, installations, projections and artistic interventions throughout the space.Think of it as a festival in club night format, shaped by Edinburgh’s creative community.

Summerhall’s Dissection Room, a former veterinary anatomy theatre where animals were once examined and studied, now transformed into one of the city’s most striking performance spaces. Now a hub for experimental arts, music and performance, Summerhall has become synonymous with boundary-pushing programming and unconventional venues. This night taps directly into that spirit: a rave rooted in history, culture and space.

This is a safe space. We will not tolerate any form of discrimitation. If you feel uncomfortable or notice an unpleasant situation, do not hesitate to contact our team.

title: Ralph Lawson: Free Time x solescience
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Free Time is Sneaky Pete's Sunday 6-10pm DJ series with legendary and breaking artists
___________________________________________ 

Ralph Lawson has played a huge role in electronic music as a DJ, producer, founder of 20/20 Vision, original resident DJ at the legendary Back to Basics club night and founder member of 2020 Soundsystem. Through it all Lawson has stayed true to the original spirit of acid house and retained a love of cutting-edge underground music in a multi-award career spanning over three decades. 

Alongside Basics, Lawson has maintained long-standing DJ residencies at The Loft Barcelona, Space Ibiza and appeared regularly at fabric London since its inception – as well as The Hacienda, Sub Club, DC10, Warehouse Project, Watergate and Panorama Bar. Lawson is also no stranger to big festival stages with performances at Sonar, Glastonbury, Love International, Houghton, Robot Heart, Burning Man, Electric Elephant, Hideout, Festival No.6, Lovebox, The Garden, Gottwood, Bluedot, Beatherder among many others.

Lawson has been behind many iconic productions over the years, often working in the studio alongside Carl Finlow, including their classic series of remixes for Blaze including ‘Lovelee Dae’ and the seminal ‘Raptures of the Deep’, both widely regarded as classic moments in house music. Lawson also collaborated with Detroit’s Chez Damier on several releases for Prescription Underground before co-founding 2020 Soundsystem, a pioneering band that fused electronic beats with live musicians and produced the highly acclaimed albums ‘No Order’ and ‘Falling’ which garnered the club hits ‘Sliding Away’ ‘Ocean’ and ‘Psycho’.

title: Aquelarre: Kulku + Vexations + Pako Vega
location: The Wee Red Bar, Edinburgh College of Art, 74 Lauriston Place; Edinburgh, EH3 9DF; Scotland
description:
Aquelarre is back with a very special international booking: Kulku, a seven-piece band travelling all the way from Germany, and emerging local band Vexations.

Kulku embodies an exhilarating Happening, a profound ceremony of raw music. Organic, driven, grounded yet floating. Kulku was founded by Johanna Riska and Andreas Riska alias Wenzlovar. The roots of their music grew out of influences from early minimal music (Moondog, Steve Reich), ritual music and art rock legends of the 60s. In the tradition of German Krautrock bands such as Can and Faust, the band is characterized by a sound whose raw material is made up of Orff instruments. With this special collection of instruments: metallophone, drums, orchestral timpani, xylophone, clarinet, saxophone, cello and an assortment of self-made instruments a universe of metaphysical stories is created. Stories about enlightened dogs or lost visionaries in the urban jungle full of light and shadows. An acoustic perpetuummobile that is fed by concentrated absorption. Exploring the sound spectrum of wood, metal and the human voice. Kulku calls their music No Age, a mix of unkitsch spirituality and the raw energy of punk/no wave.

Named after composer Satie’s infamous composition, that was banned because it was deemed too unsettling to perform live, Vexations create frenetic and off-kilter post-psych, with dissonant layered guitars, unorthodox song structures, and vocals that oscillate between spoken word and emphatic screams, to give a punchy yet unearthly sonic experience.


Pako Vega, the mind behind Aquelarre and Sounds of Ogigia, will open the night with a very special DJ set, diving into gnarly experimental sounds from avant-garde to art-rock and post-punk.

title: Resurrection
location: Usher Hall, Edinburgh, Usher Hall, Lothian Road, Edinburgh, EH1 2EA
description:
Celebrate the greatest event in history!

Join us for RESURRECTION, an event that has become an Easter tradition in Scotland, where Christians from across the central belt come together to celebrate the resurrection of Jesus through a powerful evening of music and worship.

Held annually in the iconic Usher Hall, home to the world-renowned Edinburgh Festival, this event is a joyous proclamation of the central truth of the Gospel: Christ is risen.

Led by the Origin Scotland band, choir, and orchestra, RESURRECTION unites believers from all denominations in a time of heartfelt praise and worship. It's more than just a concert; it's a gathering of the Church, where voices rise as one in celebration of the hope and victory we find in the resurrection of Jesus.

Join us this Easter Sunday for an evening that will renew your faith, fill you with joy, and unite us all around the greatest truth of our faith. Let the music move you, the message inspire you, and the atmosphere uplift you as we declare to the world the glory of the risen Christ.

title: Temporal - Tre Reynolds
location: The Liquid Room, 9c Victoria Street; Edinburgh, EH1 2HE; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
For this chapter, we welcome Tre Reynolds, one of the UK’s most formidable rising talents. Tre represents the perfect intersection of House music’s heritage and its future, blending his roots in 90s rave culture with a modern, razor-sharp precision. Known for his infectious, groove-heavy percussion and signature vocal-led Tech House, Tre’s sound is built for the sweat-soaked walls of an institution like ours.
Closing the night is Dublin-based DJ and producer Danny P is rapidly emerging as one of Ireland’s most promising new talents, with his sound earning him attention across the country’s thriving underground scene and beyond. Danny made his mark with the release of his debut two-track EP with Finn Penny on Davies Records, before breaking through with “Jump Higher”, his collaboration with Riordan on TSZR, which has already
surpassed one million streams since its release in September 2025. The track’s success has cemented his reputation as a rising force in the new wave of Irish electronic artists.
With a growing list of unreleased collaborations gaining support from the likes of Obskür, Kettama, Chris Stussy, Robbie Doherty, Gaskin, Max Dean, Ellia Jaya, and Danny Howard, Danny’s productions are reaching dance floors around the world. Having already gained some key show supports with Riordan and Us Two, he continues to build serious momentum as he steps firmly into the spotlight as one of Dublin’s most
exciting new names in house music.
This isn't just about the headliners; it’s about the community. We are rounding out the night with a handpicked selection of local DJs and Selectors who have been instrumental in shaping the current Edinburgh scene. These artists will be digging deep to set the pace, ensuring the energy remains driving and distinct from the first beat to the last.

Expect a masterclass in the groove as we delve into the depths of your temporal lobe to bring the soul of modern House back to the forefront

title: Perel at discotia
location: Sneaky Pete's, 73 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JW; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
The disco/techno heat of Perel's releases on LCD Soundsystem's DFA label are matched by her genuinely thrilling DJ Sets. Drawing on her early love for brooding New Wave and Post-Punk, she's made seminal records on Running Back and Kompakt too. Berlin House Zenner, and closing sets at Sonar Barcelona and its Lisbon edition. She's known to play Koko and Berghain's Panorama Bar, and supported the Chemical Brothers.

For as long as she can remember, Annegret Fiedler has created electronic melodies to soundtrack her daydreams. Drawing on her early love for brooding New Wave and Post-Punk, she became Perel, an electronic producer, vocalist and DJ who spent much of the 2010s shaping her sound – and to just praise. As well as being the first German artist to sign to James Murphy’s seminal dance-punk label DFA Records, Perel has performed internationally as a live act and DJ, from the floors of throbbing techno clubs to major city festivals.

Growing up in the East German state of Saxony, she learned how to write and produce songs, inspired by Aphex Twin, Kraftwerk and The Cure, among others. Later drawn to Berlin and its underground club scene, she took on the persona of Perel, writing, producing and singing on solo material for labels like Running Back, Uncanny Valley, Kompakt and Permanent Vacation. In an ever-evolving club scene with myriad faces, Perel stood out by singing over her DJ sets, bridging the mood of her own live sound with her selections.

Perel’s live performances and DJ shows are a testament to her captivating stage presence and musical versatility. Highlights include performances at Outside Lands in San Francisco, her first hard ticket selling show in Berlin House Zenner, and closing sets at Sonar Barcelona and its Lisbon edition. She has also graced stages at Nation Of Gondwana, Fusion Festival, Koko, and the renowned Panorama Bar / Berghain, as well as support to the Chemical Brothers.

Now with a raft of new music out in 2025 and a recent move to Los Angeles she continues to evolve her timeless Neosynth to enjoy as the sun sets over the horizon and the freaks come out to play.

title: Terminal V Festival 2026
location: Royal Highland Centre, Ingliston, Edinburgh, EH28 8NB
description:
Scotland's No. 1 electronic music festival, Terminal V Festival returns on April 18 + 19th 2026 with their much anticipated festival format…

Marking two epic days in the Scottish capital alongside 40,000 ravers and six fully immersive indoor and outdoor stages, the weekend is set to shake the foundations of Edinburgh's Royal Highland & Showground thanks to its diverse lineup which spans the spectrum of techno & beyond. Think new faces, fast rising talent and returning favourites.

Over 100 artists from every corner of the globe will be touching down in Edinburgh for the momentous occasion. Every stage will offer it's own standalone world, with bespoke architecture, mind-bending visuals and chest-rattling sound for a sensory experience built for those who live and breathe dance music.

Sara Landry brings the UK debut of her ETERNALISM show, her only Scottish appearance all year, while the likes of Klangkuenstler brings his unmistakable schranz sound back to the country for the first time since 2022 - a rare UK appearance.

Also joining the lineup is I Hate Models who marks a highly anticipated return alongside the likes of Clara Cuvé, Ben Hemsley, Robert Hood, Novah, Santøs, Patrick Mason, Vieze Asbak, East End Dubs, Patrick Topping, Mall Grab, Morgan Seatree, Klofama + MANY more.

Full day and stage splits have now been revealed for the festival. Each stage is designed with a clear purpose, allowing different parts of the electronic spectrum to exist without compromise, from peak-time headline techno to foundational house, from high-pressure intensity to emerging local voices.
 
Alongside a multi-faceted lineup and the addition of an extra 6th stage, a landmark £1 million investment into site development and production upgrades is in place for 2026. Following feedback, this is a transformative step designed to elevate every aspect of the festival experience. These improvements will roll out across all stages, facilities and audience areas and underpin Terminal V’s continued rise as one of Europe’s leading large-scale electronic music festivals. 

Consistently ranked among DJ Mag’s Best Festivals in the World, Terminal V continues to evolve at a pace matched only by the scene itself, which is why 40,000 people from all over the world travel in numbers to the festival.

title: Hobbes Music 13th Birthday: Michelle Manetti ⥊ Mairi B b2b Hobbes + many more ⥊ Club_Nacht
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Join us this April as Hobbes Music marks 13 years in the game. We’re celebrating properly at The Bongo Club, with special guest and label alumnus Michelle Manetti.

DJ, vocalist, producer and promoter Michelle Manetti has deep roots in UK club culture. Cutting their teeth as an underage raver in the late 90s, they’ve since become a respected figure in East London’s queer underground, bringing over 20 years of experience behind the decks and a reputation built on consistency, taste and graft rather than hype.

A committed advocate for marginalised and under-represented artists, Michelle is the founder of the FLINTA*-focused party Fèmmme Fraîche at Dalston Superstore, a long-running bi-monthly celebrating 10 years this year, known for safe space, strong line-ups and a genuine sense of community. They’ve also held residencies at The Cause and Adonis, with regular appearances at Horse Meat Disco, Little Gay Brother, Homoelectric, Percolate and beyond.

More recently, Michelle has been working closely with Demi Riquisimo, with releases including the No Given Time EP and the 2023 standout “All I Need”, a track that’s done the rounds and will already feel familiar to many. If it’s not already in your listening pile, now’s the time to fix that. Track below! 

Michelle was also previously the vocalist in cult London trio The Off Key Hat, who released a slew of head-turning records via Andy Blake's legendary vinyl-only label, Dissident (late 00s), including the bona fide club hit, Emergency Calling. MANY moons later, a pair of stellar demos landed at Hobbes Music hq and the label released the killer double A-side single, Shot At Dawn b/w Warm Your Love, in 2018.  

Michelle last visited Bongo when they co-headlined the HM label's 5th birthday with Glasgow's adopted Slovenian star (and fellow HM-alumnus), Nightwave. Suffice to say, Michelle's closing set absolutely tore the roof off the joint and they went on to deliver the goods again the following night at Paradise Palms.  Suffice to say, we've been working on a return date for some time...

Support duties on the night come from HM label boss Hobbes, going back-to-back with Mairi B to mark the label anniversary in proper fashion. Making the trip up from Newcastle, Mairi is one half of label affiliates Accident Machine, as well as a fierce selector in her own right.

Additional support acts are still to be announced, with more names landing soon.

Celebrating 31 years of NSA with

French-born Jen Cardini has been shaping dance floors for over three decades, from sweaty underground raves to Ibiza superclubs and offbeat warehouse parties. A regular at renowned clubs like Berghain, Bassiani, and Circoloco, and festivals including Glastonbury, Sonar, and Primavera, Cardini’s presence is both timeless and ever-evolving and is one of electronic music's true pioneer's.

Logan Fisher has been an important force in the UK's underground scene for over 30 years. Resident at the legendary Gardening Club in Covent Garden, London, where he ran the hedonistic Betty Ford Clinic, followed by curating the cult club night, Bumpy Capers at Plastic People and more recently the UK's hottest underground party Tryouts For The Human Race.

title: RIVAL CONSOLES
location: The Liquid Room, 9c Victoria Street; Edinburgh, EH1 2HE; Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
One of the UK’s most influential creators of electronic music, Rival Consoles, returns with a brand new audio-visual live show, centred around his new album Landscape from Memory — marking a new chapter in an ongoing quest for refinement and evolution.

London-based producer Ryan Lee West, better known under the name Rival Consoles, is notable for making synthesisers sound human and atmospheric. Over the course of a critically acclaimed eighteen-year career, his music has diversified from the challenging electronic output of his early EPs, to gradually becoming more conceptual and metamorphic with his albums.

In July 2025 Rival Consoles released his latest studio album Landscape from Memory on Erased Tapes, and is presenting it with a brand new unique performance that will take the form of an immersive audio-visual experience in which he will present material from across the last 15 years of his career and beyond. From deep ambient works to his unique version of techno, and traversing the vast spectrum in between, the show will be a long-form narrative of ideas — many heard live for the first time — with visuals co-created and performed live by Sky Ainsbury.

“Impossible to ignore, and hard to forget” — Pitchfork “Ambient leaning, mind expanding electronics” — DJ Mag

This show is 14+ / Under 16's admitted with adult

title: Max Cooper
location: Usher Hall, Edinburgh, Usher Hall, Lothian Road, Edinburgh, EH1 2EA
description:
Max Cooper is an electronic composer, multi-disciplinary artist, music label founder and former scientist who has carved out a unique space in music and visual art.

 

He holds a PhD in computational biology at the same time as being the first contemporary electronic musician to perform at the ancient Acropolis theatre in Athens and one of the first clutch of musicians to produce in Dolby Atmos.

 

Cooper has enquired for over 15 years through music, collaborations, and his label Mesh, to explore the intersections between the arts and sciences with installations, performances, immersive experiences, online media, music videos and live events. Anchored throughout is his emotive approach, connecting how ideas and forms feel, in a manner accessible to us all.

 

"I've always had a strong emotive bond with aesthetics, and found at an early age that the purified forms of electronic music carried a lot of weight for me, as did the reductive natural aesthetics of the sciences. That felt connection to both opened up a world I've been lost in ever since."


Pivoting from computational biology to electronic music might not seem like the most natural career transition, but for Cooper the commonalities are obvious:"In both fields, you're in a sense, free. Free from the limits of living systems, free from the limits of corporeal sound. The only constraints are in your mind."



Max Cooper has collaborated with Zaha Hadid Architects, Dolby, L-Acoustics, 4DSOUND, Barbican Centre, W1 Curates, The Babraham Institute and exhibited work at museums including the Pompidou Metz, ZKM Karlsruhe, Museum Wave Seoul and The Science Museum London.

title: Feel The Real (Daytime): 6th Borough Project, Frederick
location: People's Leisure Club, 45 Lothian Street, Edinburgh, EH1 1HB
description:
On Sunday May 24th, 6th Borough Project join Frederick for a daytime Bank Holiday Special! It's the first back-to-back DJ set Craig Smith & The Revenge have played together in Edinburgh for 10 years. 

The 6th Borough Project (Craig Smith & The Revenge) have worked together in the studio for over 25 years, crafting quality house jams and exploring long forgotten funk and dusty disco dubs. Their output on the revered Delusions Of Grandeur label, reinforced by their extended genre-spanning back-to-back DJ sets has helped carve their reputation as trusted producers and selectors.

Joining them is Frederick, an Edinburgh-based DJ and producer whose sound has been shaped over 15 years of close mentorship with Craig Smith. Alongside this, his ongoing collaboration with Osunalde has further informed his approach, grounding his music in rhythm, spirit and depth.

Set within Edinburgh’s newest basement venue — an intimate 200-capacity space in the city centre brought to life by Paradise Palms, Kelburn Arts and The Gilded Balloon — this is a room designed for total immersion.

Step onto one of the city’s most open and welcoming dance floors, where genuine music lovers come together. Secure your tickets and dive into a night of deep house jams, long forgotten funk and dusty disco dubs.

description:
DAYS returns to the Granton waterfront this May. We’re heading back to The Pitt Market, taking over the warehouse and yard for our best party to date.

We’ve been piecing this lineup together for a while, and the result is a proper cross-section of the sounds we’re into right now. We’ve got the heavyweights covered with Daniel Avery, DJ Seinfeld, and Helena Hauff, while Sweely brings his signature live energy. Rounding things out are Papa Nugs and MarcelDune, two selectors currently at the top of their game.

As always, the local scene is the backbone of the festival. We’ve invited a massive stack of talent from Edinburgh and further afield to make sure the energy is right from the very first set.

title: Start Of Summer Shindig: OOFT! ⥊ Ann Tweak ⥊ Hobbes ⥊ Cosmico + more ⥊ Club_Nacht
location: The Bongo Club, 66 Cowgate; Edinburgh, EH1 1JX;  Scotland; United Kingdom
description:
Summer officially starts here. On Saturday 20th June, ahead of the summer solstice, we’re pleased to welcome back our Start Of Summer Shindig, this time with a very special guest: OOFT!.

A true mainstay of Scotland’s underground for well over a decade, OOFT! (aka Ali Herron) has earned his reputation through consistently strong parties, releases and DJ sets rooted in house, disco, funk and Balearic sounds. A DJ first and foremost, his approach is musical unflashy, and forever in the know.... the kind of selector who knows how exactly to let a record breathe.

As a DJ he has held long-running residencies at some of Glasgow's top venues including theThe Sub Club, The Berkeley Suite and, more recently, weekly Sundays at La Cheetah for his KEEP ON residency with fellow Gtown veteran David Barbarossa. Beyond Glasgow, he’s toured extensively across the UK, Europe and further afield, bringing a sound that comfortably moves from slow-motion Balearic moods through underground disco to classic, soulful house.

In the studio, OOFT! has built a deep and varied catalogue, releasing on respected international labels and carving out a reputation for music that DJs actually play. His 2012 track 'Love & Soulsaving' became something of a modern underground classic moving effortlessly through DJ bags worldwide, clocking up close to three and a half million streams and selling out its vinyl run, copies of which now regularly fetch serious money second-hand.

Since the release of his debut album Intricacies Of Modern Life in 2018, the focus has shifted. While original productions continue, much of his studio time is now spent crafting bespoke edits, reworks and tweaks - tools designed purely for the dancefloor. Many are cut exclusively for his own sets, tested in the moment and never released publicly. It’s this behind-the-scenes graft - the quiet refinement of records you didn’t know you needed - that keeps OOFT! firmly in demand and endlessly trusted behind the decks.

⥊
